purpose: Decomposes GDP growth into labor quantity, labor quality, capital deepening, and total factor productivity to assess potential GDP and the sustainability of current growth rates.
components: Labor force growth (including immigration contribution of 0.2-0.5pp)
Total factor productivity (~1.5-2% aggregate, no AI structural break)
current_assessment: Immigration has contributed 0.2-0.5pp annually to potential GDP growth via extensive margin. AI has not yet produced a measurable TFP structural break at the aggregate level. The forward trajectory depends on immigration policy persistence and AI diffusion timeline — both highly uncertain.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between cyclical TFP variation and structural shifts in real time
Immigration contribution is politically contingent and may reverse
Does not incorporate hysteresis effects (demand shaping supply)
Potential GDP estimates are subject to the same 1.0-2.0pp revision problem as the output gap
